title: herniaria ciliolata subsp. subciliata
intro: Herniaria ciliolata subsp. subciliata, also known as small rampion or rampion, is a type of plant that belongs to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Herniaria ciliolata subsp. subciliata include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is also used as a topical application for skin conditions and wounds.
